current_suggestion.pitch: Levin is a biologist at Tufts whose work on bioelectricity, morphogenesis, and cognitive science sits at the intersection of biology, AI, and philosophy of mind — none of your current channels touch this territory. His core argument is that intelligence and goal-directedness are substrate-independent and exist at every level of biology, which directly steelmans a perspective missing from your carnivore/longevity stack and adds rigorous depth to your AI capability interests by asking what intelligence actually is. Mostly solo lectures and interview appearances, running 30–90 minutes, dense with empirical content and genuine conceptual challenge.
